$comment: Shared severity-escalation table for the pr-review gate. Consumed by both the Codex $pr-review skill (read alongside review-criteria.md during severity normalization) and the Claude-side /pr-review skill (parsed and passed into the pr-review dynamic workflow). Canonical copy: private_dot_codex/skills/pr-review/references/severity-rules.json — the copy under ~/.claude/skills/pr-review/references/ is generated by chezmoi from this file; edit only the canonical copy. Prose gate policy (modes, acceptance, caps rationale, re-review): review-criteria.md.

critical.$comment: A finding is Critical when it matches ANY rule in any_of AND satisfies the guard.
critical.guard: The finding explains a concrete merge-blocking risk grounded in the committed branch diff, sets blocking=yes, identifies impact_scope, and relies only on verified_assumptions with no unverified_assumptions needed for the blocker claim. Single-specialist Critical labels are candidates, not final severity.

downgrade_to_important.$comment: Machine-readable Critical downgrade policy. If impact_scope matches impact_scope_patterns, downgrade the Critical candidate unless an override pattern also matches. Assumption-dependent candidates are still rejected by critical.guard.

important.$comment: Evaluated only for findings that did not qualify as Critical. A finding is Important when it matches ANY rule in any_of AND satisfies the guard.
important.guard: The finding is likely worth fixing before merge but is not a proven blocker. Critical candidates that fail the Critical guard because impact is local-only, advisory, ignored-state-only, or assumption-dependent should usually land here when the risk is still concrete.

suggestion.rule: Remaining advisory findings are Suggestions unless the specialist explicitly marks them as positive observations (Strengths).

nit.rule: Style-only, preference-only, naming-only, or speculative cleanup. Never promote nits, style preferences, speculative rewrites, or weakly grounded concerns into Critical or Important; nits do not enter the fix queue.

incomplete_evidence: If evidence is incomplete but the risk may be severe, keep the finding with the missing verification stated explicitly; do not silently drop it. Do not classify it as Critical unless the blocker claim itself is already supported by verified assumptions.
